citations.narrative:
The licensee did not keep records of treatment procedures performed for all dogs at the facility. Medical records show that
a veterinarian prescribed antibiotic treatment for a six-year-old female Beagle (tattoo POC-1), but there are no records
showing that the treatments were given. The veterinarian diagnosed that the beagle had interdigital cysts on January 5,
2026, and prescribed a treatment of topical antibiotic ointment and oral antibiotics to be given “until better”. At the time of
inspection, the beagle’s enclosure has colored tabs on it which indicate that she is still under treatment and reminding
staff to give the medications, but there is no record that confirms that the treatments were given. Complete medical
records, including records confirming that prescribed treatments are performed, are important to ensure that the
veterinarian’s directives are being followed. Dealers and research facilities must keep such medical records and make
them available for APHIS inspection.
To be corrected by: February 6, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian.
Additional Inspectors:
Alexandra Friedman, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
3.13(b)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The licensee did not keep records of treatment procedures performed for all dogs at the facility. Medical records show that 
a veterinarian prescribed antibiotic treatment for a six-year-old female Beagle (tattoo POC-1), but there are no records 
showing that the treatments were given. The veterinarian diagnosed that the beagle had interdigital cysts on January 5, 
2026, and prescribed a treatment of topical antibiotic ointment and oral antibiotics to be given “until better”. At the time of 
inspection, the beagle’s enclosure has colored tabs on it which indicate that she is still under treatment and reminding 
staff to give the medications, but there is no record that confirms that the treatments were given. Complete medical 
records, including records confirming that prescribed treatments are performed, are important to ensure that the 
veterinarian’s directives are being followed. Dealers and research facilities must keep such medical records and make 
them available for APHIS inspection. 
To be corrected by: February 6, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Alexandra Friedman, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
